title: Realmz.io Docs
content:
## Main


Realmz.io Docs 

Version Control:
Updates: Semver

Github Branches:
Main: latest version of the game, where all features branches are merged when finished
Production: the code that's live, AUTOMATICALLY UPDATES SERVERS
Feature branches - branches with any name where 1 or 2 people work on a feature

Style Guidelines:

Variables: camelCase
Classes: UpperCamelCase
Tabs: 4 spaces

From now on, add docstrings to new functions or classes (not that it’s gonna help with the old code)

Employee table




## Setup & Dev Tools

Development Setup:

Clone
Create a .env

  (replace red part)

npm i
node index.js

Visit http://localhost:3000/
Dev hub: http://localhost:3000/dev


Run React Client (DEVELOPMENT MODe) 
Create 2 separate terminal tabs (Windows Powershell lets you do this)

Npm run start runs the realmz.io game and account API on port 3000
Npm run start runs the React server on port 5173

Visit http://localhost:5173/ for new UI dev version

Pushing:

Notes: 
git add . will add everything in the folder you’re in and subfolders. Ideally go to the root, iogame4
Only push to origin when you tested your changes and they are ready for team members to merge

Uncommit without deleting stuff: git reset --soft HEAD~1

Mapmaker & Creating maps:

Go to ./createMap.js
Edit the object at the top to include the map width and height and a unique name
Limits:
2 byte position width: (2**28 - 1)/100 tiles)
3 byte position width: ~160 tiles width

run  node createMap.js

Go to config.js and change the mapmaker file to the name 

Visit https://localhost:3000/mapmaker to edit maps
If tiles are invisible, click “fix” at the left. 
Ctrl+S is needed to save progress in certain modes 

Item Spritesheet Generation

npm run itemgen

Image Viewer

https://localhost:3000/dev/image/imageviewer.html 


## Client-Server Architecture
Server arch diagram:



Database: MongoDB on the cloud

Game server:


Server - Client:


Game Loop (20 TPS):

update players
update game bots
update player weapons	
update projectiles
	find deleted and new projectiles
update mobs
update all entity physics
update xp orbs
build orb packets for each world
for every player, build update packet
send packets

Backend:
Files:
Root directory - game.js, index.js
/db - files for interacting with the database
/server - all HTTP routes and account API, client building
/lib - all core game files
/maps - old text based maps, not used
/maps_new - all maps in the new format
/gamemodes - specific logic for gamemodes
Shared:
Shared data between client and server includes skins, mob info, mission info, items
Note: weapon.json is NOT used
Shop.json is temp, it’s actually stored in the database
Client: 
Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS

Client Building:
index.html and the scripts that go with it are automatically combined together in server/buildClient.js when the server serves the main page ( HTTP GET / )
In the development environment, client files are separate for debugging
In production, they are combined and obfuscated 
If you add a new client file, add it in the the getScripts() function at the top of buildClient.js, and make sure the dependency order is correct
Files:
/public - all static assets (html, images, sounds, styles)
/dev/scripts/… - all the client files, details below

Files: 
accounts.js - client side logic for UI / Account API
audio.js -  audio playing 
biomes.js - rendering data for all biomes in the game
main.js - a bunch of the main logic thrown together - all websocket logic
mainRenderLoop.js - main render loop
map.js - all functions for map rendering, parsing, etc
predict.js - EXPERIMENTAL FILE - not actually used
render.js - tons of render logic for everything
renderAccessories.js - specific rendering logic for accessory effects
renderMobs.js - specific rendering logic for mobs
renderGL.js - EXPERIMENTAL FILE - not actually used
utils.js - utils
weaponGUI.js - logic for weapon tree popup (hold tab in game)
weaponInfo.js - tooltips for weapons, not used anymore
weatherManager.js - weather effects on client & rendering
ws.js - websocket class


## Plans


 











Optimizations:
Rendering on worker thread 
Attempt client side prediction (client side refactor)  
Packet buffering OR movement correction & masking


Mobile:


Art:
redo


Devtools (low priority):
Mapmaker update
Remove spawns mode
Item map generator (low priority) 
Mob animator (maybe) (low priority) 


Early 2024 Summarized data:
New Content & Features: Many players requested new weapons/upgrades (e.g., lvl 70 upgrades for scythe, alchemist, aura monk, mirror), more bosses (e.g., slime, gnome, castaway as final boss, zombie boss), and new gamemodes (e.g., adventure mode, team battles, survival mode).
Quality of Life & Gameplay Improvements: Players asked for better in-game information (e.g., interactive tutorial, seeing missions in-game, details to missions, map legend), a change username feature, a clan creation page, and private rooms/servers with admin commands.
Balance & Difficulty Adjustments: Several players suggested making the bosses harder (Ancient Statue, Mechanical Terror, Goblin Warlock) or easier (Goblin Warlock, Erthium Eye), and asked for buffs to ranged projectile weapons and nerfs to the "small" upgrade. They also asked for adjustments to the death level penalty and the XP from stars.
Technical & Development Questions: People were curious about the next major update (2.8), the game's lore, the use of a custom game engine, the Arctic portal's destination, how to become a mod or developer, and questions about hackers (e.g., how to become one, how to become an anti-hacker, and why they are in the game).
Tournament & Event Information: Questions were asked about when the tournament (Realmzfest) will happen (e.g., weekends), the prize money distribution (e.g., without personal info), and how to request other weapons for the tournament.



## Guides
Guide: adding weapons
Go to /lib/weapons/X.js to start making the weapon class to define behavior

If the weapon creates projectiles copy this (blue describes properties of weapon and projectile created):

class CLASS extends Ranged {
    constructor(owner) {
        super("NAME", owner, COOLDOWN_MS, DAMAGE, VELOCITY, PIERCE, "proj_spit2", RADIUS, {
 color: "#ef901c", xoff: 40, yoff: -10 });
        this.angle = new Animation(0 + 90, 12, 24);
        this.angle.autoSetAnimation(135, 0 + 90);
        this.angle.bindObject(this.attacking, false, 23);
        this.xoff = 40; //offset  from player where projectile is made (copy this into the projectile options object)
        this.yoff = -10; // same as above but for Y
    }
    useWeapon(Game) {
        this.angle.start();
        this.createProjectile(Game);
    }
}

If it’s melee copy this:

class CLASS extends Melee {
    constructor(owner) {
        super("NAME", owner, COOLDOWN_MS, DAMAGE, KNOCKBACK, [HITBOX ARRAY], { x: 35, y: 20 });
        this.angle = new Animation(0 + 90, 8, 16); //INITIAL VALUE, keyframe ticks (so starts at 90 deg, has keyframes at 8 and 16 ticks)
        this.angle.autoSetAnimation(180, 0 + 90); //At tick 8, at 180 degrees, at tick 16, at 90 degrees 
        this.angle.bindObject(this.contactCollision, true, 0); //hitbox starts collisions at animation tick 0
        this.angle.bindObject(this.contactCollision, false, 10);  //hitbox ends collisions at tick 10 
        this.angle.bindObject(this.attacking, true, 0); //is attacking at tick 0 
        this.angle.bindObject(this.attacking, false, 15); //this.attacking just lets you turn weapon but doesn’t mean u can use it
        this.ogAngle = 0;

    }
    useWeapon() {
        this.angle.start();
    }
}

Functions to overload:
useWeapon():
Specify which animations start (this.angle.start(), this.x.start())
Look at how similar weapons are made and copy them 

Then at the bottom of the file add it into the exports
Example:
module.exports = {
    Shield: Shield,
    Mirror: Mirror,
    Swordsman: Warrior,
    Sabre: Sabre,
    Katana: Katana,
    Excalibur: Excalibur,
    "Death Sabre": DeathSabre,
    Behemoth: Behemoth,
    "Slasher": Minimoth,
    TestWeapon: CLASS
}

Then in /lib/weapon.js you can add it into the tree or just type /weapon [NAME] to test it in game: 
Example: /weapon TestWeapon 
(note you use the export name, not the name given in the class or the class name.)
Class name used for: in the files
Export name used for: outside the file or reference it in game commands
Name given in constructor: Display name in game

Adding sounds: /shared/weapons.json 
Copy other weapons but only have “sound” property because that's the only one that does something. 
Use any name from /public/sounds (ex: “whoosh”, “bow”) (if you add a sound, make sure to add it to the download in /dev/scripts/main.js)

Client side:
/dev/scripts/weaponGUI.js
Copy paste the weapon tree into it (so annoying bruh) (COPY PASTE EXACT TREE FROM SERVER’S WEAPON.JS)
At bottom add it to weaponIndex following the pattern.
Add its icon in the itemmap.png (size 50x50 px)
/dev/scripts/render.js
Create function for rendering (copy paste most similar class function)
Add it to the top in const weapons (use the name you put inside weapon class constructor)
Tweak the function till it works
Tip: uncomment line 85 AND 86 in /lib/weapons/classes/melee.js to debug collisions - remember to disable it tho
Guide: adding upgrades
[outdated]
/lib/entities/player.js look around and figure it out - you just edit add upgrade and the upgrade list at bottom
/dev/scripts/main.js find uInfo and then add color and description (colors are css classnames)
Guide: adding skins
Upload skin to /public/images/skins
Add it to /shared/skins.json
Edit /db/db.js if new chest
Guide: adding mobs
To edit current mob properties, go to /shared/mobs.json
Edit AI in /lib/mob-ai.js
Edit mob class in /lib/mob.js

/dev/scripts/renderMobs.js - just copy similar mob code


## Notes
jump more better
Tiny tap = small jump
Hold key = big jump
Feedback for movement
Numbers under upgrades
Reload indicator 


## Entities
class Entity


class Player extends Entity


class Mob extends Entity


Movement (for Entity)
Client 
Entity.prototype.MOVEMENT_TYPE
0: normal walking & jumping movement
1: flying movement 
All movement related functions (setKeys, doJump, moveX) must be called by 
movementFunction.bind(entity)(args...)



## Commands
Admin Commands
/xp → +50k xp
/xpxp → +500k xp
/setxpxp → +500k weapon xp
/xp123 → Set absurd xp
/playground → Switch to playground map
/crashtest → Emit console "crashtest"



## Game
Game Scale



## DEV: Epics
Epic ID: Clan-1: Users can battle other clans in skirmishes
User Story 1:
As a clan member, I can enter a new map/server when a skirmish starts to battle the enemy clan
User Story 2:
As a clan leader, I can start an invasion of an enemy province in order to start a skirmish
User Story 3:
As a player in the skirmish, I can use upgrades gained by my clan to have more weapons to battle the enemy with.
User Story 4:
As a winner of the skirmish, I can unlock new upgrades and provinces for my clan in order for them to become stronger

Epic ID: Clan-2: Clan Skirmish Scheduling System 
//Polling system psuedocode for auto scheduling
User Story 1:
As a defending clan leader, I can send a scheduling request to the enemy clan in order to find a time where users are available to fight
User Story 2:
As an attacking clan member, I can accept/deny timed requests in order to fight at a scheduled time.
User Story 3:
As a clan member, I can interact with attack/defense alerts to instantly join a new skirmish.

Epic ID: Clan-3: Clans can be ranked and level up based on performance
User Story 1:
As a clan member, I can gain EXP from the amount of provinces my clan owns in order to get stronger weapons
User Story 2:
As a clan leader, I can gain access to clan upgrades depending on my clan's performance and the number of provinces owned.

Epic ID: Clan-4: Clans can be managed and have differing roles
User Story 1:
As a clan leader I can manage/edit the roles of existing clan members in order for there to be more structure within the clan.
User Story 2:
As a player, I can create a new clan by buying it in order to start a clan of my own.
User Story 3:
As a player, I can request to join a new clan by searching the clan name/tag.
User Story 4:
As a clan leader, I can accept/deny new join requests from players in order to gain members.
User Story 5:
As a clan administrator, I can assign new roles to players, as well as kick members in order to manage the structure of the clan.


## Issue C11
Objective
Provide a system to rank clans based on performance of players in normal servers
Requirements
update clan schema to include a ranking
function to calculate rankings
getClanLeaderboard route USING new leaderboard system
Ideas
ELO system based on clans killing other clans in game?
Average kills/stars/etc...
Multiple rankings, then weigh them to calculate final ranking??
Potential for ELO calculation ( in order to prevent abuse ? ) :

Only increase ELO for kills on other players WITH accounts in order to try and mitigate players abusing alts in order to farm ELOPlayers should only be awarded ELO for kills if:
The ELO of the player they killed is <=20% (arbitrary value) of their own, or greater
The player level of the player they killed is also similar, <=20% of their own, or greater
The player is outside of their clan

The amount of ELO awarded should be the same amount of ELO the losing player loses, except in cases of where their ELO falls outside of the range as stated above. 
This could help cases of weird clan in-fighting or trades in order to artificially boost ELO, as the amount gained / lost would be equivalent (?)  -> to be further thought of…

Average clan ELO can just be the average of all the players ELO 


Properties:
Starter ELO: 1000ELO Floor: 500

 (that player has in game)
 (that player has in game)
Pitier - what class level the player is on


 expected win probability (P2 loser, P1 winner)


(players, num players), loser loses 80% 


	


## GridInventory

Inventory Docs:


cursorInventory: an inventory with only ONE slot. It's the thing a player has in their cursor/is picking up. Note: will usually be empty, but when user moves stuff around the item should go into it








Epic ID: Inventory-1: Users can manage items in their inventory.
As a user, I can add an item to my inventory, given that there is enough space.
As a user, I can remove an item from my inventory, in order to make room.
As a user, I can rearrange the items in my inventory, in any way that I would like to.
AS a user I can Boil Crab Cakes alive 


Epic ID: Inventory-2: Users can manage accessories in their inventory.
Users will have a set amount of accessory slots, up to 7, in which to put their active accessories. This will function similarly to the terarria accessory system, where they could switch their accessories in order to get their desired effects.

Users can also do a similar thing with weapons, choosing which weapon to select as their primary, equipping them from the inventory.

Users can select which consumables to have in their primary slots, for ease of access to use.

For potions/resources, they can also be used directly from the inventory.

Users can view any modifiers, and their effects in the inventory, as well as any tooltips about the base items.

## clans part 2
Wars:
A clan can declare war on another clan. Once a clan is at war with another clan, they can have a skirmish with the clan. In a war, a clan can send a surrender request to another war to give cusstomizable concessions in exchange for peace such as occupied territory and resources.

Skirmish:
Once two clans are at war, one clan can attempt to contest control of a bordering province of another clan in a skirmish. The skirmish will be a series of team battles with the winner of the skirmish taking control of the tile when the skirmish ends. The provinces can have buildings on them which can help benefit the clan such as producers of resources and defences that can assist the clan in battle.

Resources:
Stone
Wood
You get a certain amount of resources per day depending on your buildings and land you control
Buildings:
Clans will be able to build on provinces they on. Buildings will become exponentially more expensive depending on how many you own to make it hard to spam them.
Fortress (Attacking players need to win an extra round per defence to capture the province)
Lumberjack mill (You gain extra wood per day)
Stone mine (You get extra stone per day)
Gold mine (You get extra coins per day)
Erthium mine (You have a chance to get erthium)
Storage silo (increaese maximum resources)
Command Center (You need one less battle to capture a neighboring province)
Factory (Used for resource conversion)

Bank:
Each clan has a bank which contains resources and currency. People who have management permission on it can withdraw and deposit in it.
Clan Rewards:
Winning clan wars as exceeding goals for milestones of total stars will allow users to gain rewards to claim. There will also be a new currency, possibly given to encourage people to start clan wars. 

Some potential skins that are clan exclusive ^.




## WebGL Plan
ShaderManager - compile, link, cache
Texturemanager - load image, loadatlas, fbo creation, bind
Camera js - viewProjection matrix - will need this in the shaders ofc
Preload atlas textures
WebGLRenderer.js - has beginFrame, endFrame
Renderable interface (buckets for objects that use the same program)This contains the webglprogram, vao, mode, vertexBuffer, vertexData, vertexCount
Sprite
Sprite shaders, vert and frag
Spritebatcher 
Preallocate large float32array 
Dynamic vertex buffer, vao, index buffer
drawSprite(tex, x, y, w, h, ) - ideally we have texture atlases for all groups to minimize draws - increments vertexCount
flush() - conducts the memory transfer, draw, and vertex reset, so it will be called between texture sheet changes
Try to group textures
Vertex format:
			[x, y]
			[u, v]
			[r, g, b, a]


Tiles
Upload texture atlas as gpu texture
Instead of drawing to offscreen canvas we can just use Framebuffer object
